changes.emptyHint: No revertible file changes in this session yet. AI file tools or ACC writes will appear here.

permission.mode.acceptEdits.description: File edits run automatically; sensitive actions such as commands still require approval.
permission.mode.auto.confirm: Switch to “Smart auto” mode? AI will assess risk and run low-risk actions automatically. Continue?
permission.mode.auto.description: AI assesses risk; low-risk actions run automatically while high-risk actions still ask.
permission.mode.bypass.confirm: Switch to “Skip permissions” mode? The model can edit files and run commands without confirmation. Continue?
permission.mode.default.description: Ask for your approval before every action; safest option.
permission.mode.footer: File changes have checkpoints; review and revert them in Audit.
permission.mode.plan.description: AI presents a complete plan for approval before it runs.

provider.advanced: Advanced (system prompt / sampling temperature / backup endpoints)
provider.backupEndpoints: Backup endpoints (one per line, up to 3; used in order if the primary endpoint is unavailable)
provider.contextAutoHint: Leave blank to auto-detect. Switch this engine to the current engine to see the active value and source here.
provider.deleteConfirm: Delete Provider “{{name}}”? Its API key will also be removed.
provider.empty: No providers configured. Add one from a template below (DeepSeek / Tongyi Qianwen / Custom), then enter its API key.

provider.pricing.title: Unit price · Cost estimate (optional, per million tokens)
session.bulkCleanup.description: This will delete {{count}} unpinned historical chats.
session.bulkCleanup.empty: There are no historical chats to clean up
session.bulkCleanup.note: Your current chat, pinned chats, and running chats will be kept. This cannot be undone.
session.bulkCleanup.purgeAssociated: Also delete associated rollback checkpoints and Agent workflow records
session.bulkCleanup.purgeHint: Workspace files are never deleted. Clear this option to delete only the primary chat records.
session.bulkCleanup.title: Bulk history cleanup
session.empty: No chats yet. Select New chat to begin.
settings.advanced.maxToolIterations.hint: Standard tasks use this base budget (default: 100). Long tasks start at 200 and can extend to 300 while making progress.
settings.advanced.subagentConcurrency.hi: How many subagents can run simultaneously in a stage. Range: 1–8; default and upper limit: 8.
settings.advanced.subagentPerTurn.hint: Combined spawn_agent/orchestrate_agents limit for one chat turn. Set 0 to disable subagents.
settings.advanced.subagentPreferredModel: Default model id for subagents; empty = fall back to role / tier / main model.
settings.advanced.subagentPreferredProvi: Default Provider id for spawn_agent / workflow nodes (cross-provider); empty = main provider. For hard tasks the model may pick another model under the same provider (e.g. Pro) via spawn_agent.model.
settings.advanced.toolLoadingMode.hint: Adaptive mode predicts the task and loads only relevant tools; AI can search and load more when needed. It applies to both Claude CLI and OpenAI-compatible engines.
settings.advanced.workflowMaxNodes.hint: Maximum nodes in one persistent DAG workflow run (graph editor/templates). Range: 1–32; default and upper limit: 32. This is separate from the per-turn subagent budget.
settings.agentRoles.hint: The same roles work with OpenAI-compatible APIs and Claude CLI. Claude uses native --agents, and project .claude/agents are discovered automatically.
settings.anthropicEndpoint.hint: The endpoint, key, and model configured here are injected into the Claude CLI child process for real chats; no manual setx is needed. You can switch models from the top bar.
settings.anthropicEndpoint.title: Third-party Anthropic-compatible endpoint (Coding Plan)
settings.apiKeyPlaceholder: Leave blank to keep the saved key unchanged
settings.authMode.auto: Automatic (send both headers for broad endpoint compatibility)
settings.authMode.bearer: Bearer Token (ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN, for example Ark Coding Plan)
settings.betaThinking: Interleaved-thinking beta (needed only by older CLIs; do not enable for new models)
settings.bridgeExternalMcp: Bridge external MCP tools to native Provider engines too (when disabled, Providers use only built-in workbench tools)
settings.browserTarget.cdp: Connect to an existing browser CDP endpoint
settings.browserTarget.hint: System mode opens a new tab/window in the real user browser and never navigates, reuses, or closes the current Workbench. It operates through screenshots and UIA/OCR; choose managed, CDP, or bundled mode only when DOM-level browser_* tools are needed.
settings.browserTarget.managed: Installed default Chromium browser (DOM automation; separate window)
settings.browserTarget.system: System default browser (recommended; preserve Workbench and reuse user session)
settings.claudePathPlaceholder: claude.cmd or the complete path to an internal CLI
settings.commandOverridePlaceholder: Leave blank to auto-detect, or enter the full python / ai-computer-control.exe path
settings.discoverModels: Fetch available models from the endpoint automatically (GET /v1/models; fall back to the built-in list on failure)
settings.doctor.hint: Check the service, Claude CLI, Node.js, Git, search, and desktop capabilities.
settings.doctor.title: Environment diagnostics
settings.endpointApiKey: Endpoint API key (leave blank to use the system environment variable)
settings.endpointApiKeyPlaceholder: Leave blank to use the system environment variable
settings.endpointBaseUrl: Endpoint base URL (leave blank to use ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL)
settings.externalMcpServer.hint: Import an external MCP server from a folder that contains a ruyi-mcp.json manifest.
settings.extraModels: Custom models (one per line; use id or id|display name)
settings.integrations.hint: Connect the desktop-control MCP (ai-computer-control) to the workbench for screenshots, window control, OCR, and more. Claude starts it through its CLI; native Provider engines use the built-in MCP client bridge.
settings.monthlyBudget.hint: Set a monthly cost cap to show progress in Usage. Going over it shows a non-blocking reminder. Leave it blank for no budget; amounts are estimates, not actual charges.
settings.monthlyBudget.title: Monthly cost budget (optional)
settings.network.hint: Once configured, AI can search the web and automatically use the local cache while offline.
settings.permissionBridge: Bridge permission prompts (--permission-prompt-tool; requires a non-bypass permission mode)
settings.pricing.hint: If the endpoint above is a third-party Coding Plan, such as Ark, these prices are only for cost estimates when the backend supports them. Plan-included usage normally shows token consumption only; subscription billing is not usage-based billing, so do not treat it as an actual charge.
settings.pricing.unavailablePlaceholder: Leave blank to skip cost estimates
settings.providers.hint: After you select a Provider in the top bar, chats connect directly to its streaming /chat/completions endpoint. Keys are stored only in local config.json.
settings.stopTasksOnClose: Stop running tasks when closing this page
settings.takeOverPort: Take over a port held by an older instance at startup
settings.thinkingBudgetPlaceholder: Blank = adaptive (recommended for new models); a number = fixed enabled budget
settings.title: Settings

tool.artifacts.empty: No artifacts yet — files generated by AI will appear here.
tool.revertibleChanges.hint: Files written by AI file tools or desktop control appear here. Use the Changes in this turn card in Chat to revert an entire turn.
usage.budget.over: This month: about {{spent}} / budget {{budget}}, over by about {{over}} (estimate)
usage.cost.unavailable: No cost estimate (unit prices are not configured, or usage is subscription-billed)
usage.dailyTrend.summary: Daily token trend across {{count}} days; peak about {{tokens}} ({{date}}).
usage.empty: No usage records yet. Start a chat to see a cost summary here.
usage.note.cost: Cost is an equivalent estimate based on usage, not an actual charge.
usage.note.planTurns: About {{count}} turns are subscription-billed; their tokens are not included in the cost above.
usage.note.planUsage: Some usage is subscription-billed; only tokens are shown and not included in the cost above.
usage.opsInterventions: {{count}} manual interventions in the last {{days}} days
usage.planIncluded.title: Subscription plan: billed monthly; tokens do not incur separate charges.
usage.title: Usage and cost
usage.unavailable: Usage data is temporarily unavailable. Try refreshing in a moment.
workflow.connectionLost: ⚠ Connection lost, canvas data may not be current
workflow.detail.placeholder: Click a node on the canvas to view its model, progress, gate verdict, and actions.
workflow.editor.blankCreated: Created a blank template. Saving will create a new template.
workflow.editor.connectHint: Connect mode: select a target node to create a dependency arrow from the current node.
workflow.editor.copyCreated: Switched to a new template copy. Saving will not overwrite the original.
workflow.editor.defaultTask: Describe the task this node should complete
workflow.editor.delete.confirm: Delete workflow template “{{title}}”? This cannot be undone.
workflow.editor.delete.none: There is no saved personal or project copy of this template to delete
workflow.editor.loaded: Loaded the template. Saving overwrites the same personal or project ID; built-in templates save as personal copies.
workflow.editor.name: Name
workflow.editor.title: Workflow graph editor
workflow.empty: This chat has no Agent workflows yet.
workflow.empty.goChat: Go to chat to assign a task
workflow.empty.subtitle: Assign a multi-agent task and their collaboration graph will be drawn here in real time — who's running, how far, and where they're stuck.

workflow.mail.empty: No inter-agent messages yet. Sub-agents can talk via send_to_agent; they will be listed here.
workflow.pool.approvedToast: Approved — task added to the workflow
workflow.pool.cost: Cost: adds 1 node, up to ~{{iters}} iterations
workflow.pool.empty: No pool proposals yet. Running sub-agents may propose new tasks for your approval.

workflow.steerBox.noDeterministic: Deterministic gate nodes don't call the model; cannot steer
